references.title: Unveiling the Differences in Early Performance Prediction Between Online Social Sciences and STEM Courses Using Educational Data Mining

extracted.Features: Sum of Clicks per activity type.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Each activity type was aggregated from the start to current week (in this case, there are 20 activity types, so each model has 20 features representing the number of clicks in each activity.

Data was oversampled using SMOTE and Standardized (I’m guessing this is what StandardNormalization.
extracted.Models:
DT
Logistic Regression
GaussianNB
KNN
LDA
SVM
AdaBoost
Bagging
ExtraTrees
Random Forest
XGBoost
MLP
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Accuracy: 0.79 - Random Forest & ExtraTrees, Mean Rank: 10.04 - Random Forest}
extracted.Comments:
In total, the authors created 10824 models, one per course iteration and Week.

Authors publish results in terms of Accuracy, Mean Rank  and Friedman’s test. Most of the results shown are averaged out over the full period or aggregated by some week thresholds (Week 10, Week 20, Week 30, Week 40).

At a second level of analysis, the authors look to distinguish differences in Predictive capabilities between STEM and Social Science Courses and find that the models in STEM achieve better scores than the ones for SS. 

Recommendation of authors is to perform early prediction for STEM at Week 10 and for SS at week 20.
extracted.Limitations:
Each model is course-specific, making it impractical for implementation.

No analysis of casuses for difference in performance between STEM and SS. 

Accuracy is the only model performance metric considered.

annotations.text: Using learner logs to create early-warning systems is, while useful conceptually, highly dependent on knowledge-domain (because different disciplines tend to require different forms of engagement which, in turn, often leads to differences in interaction patterns).
Authors focus on creating a EWS for Social Sciences and STEMa and use data from 32593 students (seems to be OULAD). Results show dedcent accuracies starting at the 10th week.
